title.rendered: 4 Signs You Need a New Office Chair

content.rendered:
[vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text text_larger=”no”]One of the key aspects of office ergonomics is the chair you sit in everyday. Choosing the correct and suitable ergonomic chair for employees has proven various and direct benefits to productivity and wellness. An ergonomic chair is one that eliminates physical problems such as strains and discomfort caused by the extended use of a normal chair. Ergonomic chairs optimally provide the needed support for the body when sitting at a desk taking into account back support, comfort, and mobility. An ergonomic office chair also features several adjustable pieces so that it fits each user individually, allowing everyone to sit at their own standards of comfort. On the other hand, an inadequate normal chair can have a severe impact on employees’ physique and as a result their productivity and work effectiveness.

Like any other piece of office furniture, a chair will eventually need to be replaced. If you’re not sure whether it’s time to replace your chair or not, here are 4 signs to keep an eye out for:

 

1. Damaged chair

If your office chairs are cracked, ripped, or collapsed into pieces, it’s about time to find a replacement. Armrests, knobs, and levers that adjust seat height and backrest are examples of loose or non-functioning components that are commonly overlooked. If any of these components no longer allow adjustment of the chairs to support a correct posture, it’s time to look for new desk chairs.

 

2. Physical discomfort

Our bodies were not built to sit still in the same position for long periods. The most obvious sign that your chairs aren’t well-suited for you and your employees is pain. It’s time to upgrade your workspace chairs if your employees are experiencing physical pain, be it upper or lower back, shoulders, or neck. The correct chair will help you sit comfortably for long periods without experiencing any aches or pains.

 

3. Lifespan

After some time, the structural stability and the value of the furniture deteriorate to the point where they can no longer be saved. The average lifespan of an office chair is five years. If you’ve had an office chair for that long, regardless of its condition, it’s time to replace it. The reason is simple, the chair has seen it all in five years. And while it may not show it, it is just a matter of time and the chair’s condition will fall off very quickly. Rusted joints, loose bolts, and loosened metal parts that are not visible to the naked eye could also cause harm to the user.

4. Outdated design

While this doesn’t signify a pressing reason to replace your office chair based on function, industry norms do change, and you’ll need to adapt and keep your office space up to the market trends. All of today’s greatest ergonomic chairs feature some degree of flexibility and strong spinal support, while most of the old chairs were designed to be comfy at the time, and the absence of today’s ergonomic trends almost renders them obsolete.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title.rendered: Helpful Tips To Consider When Buying A Desk in Your Office Space

content.rendered:
[vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text text_larger=”no”]Employees spend majority of their time at their desks. Thus, before furnishing your office space, you need take some serious considerations to guarantee that you have a comfortable and efficiently utilized workspace. The most important factor to consider when choosing an office desk is to satisfy employees’ efficiency requirements so that they can do their job to the best of their abilities. The desk you purchase should be able to accommodate all of their work items, including a computer, office supplies, equipment -if required and depending on their job functionalities. But it must also make your office look elegant, big, and welcoming too. When searching for an office desk, keep these considerations in mind once you’ve decided on a budget and an office furnishing solution provider.

Size

How much space do you have for each department? This question will assist you in determining the size of office desks or workstations in contrast. Take into account the room’s size, if your office is huge and extravagant, a large, elegant desk will work well. For smaller spaces, however, use a more compact alternative or workstations instead. With a smaller desk, you’ll be able to fit in more office elements, such as additional chairs and storage units.

Ensure that the buffer space around the desk is convenient for utilization. A desk should be placed no less than 80 cm from the wall to enable employees to move their desk chair freely without limitations and without damaging the adjacent wall every time they stand up. Also, consider the space to the sides of the desk. The passage beside the desk should be no less than 60 cm.

Desk size is also influenced by job functions. Executive desks tend to stand between 180 cm and 250 cm including side unit and are usually placed alone in the director’s private office. For shared workspaces, the minimum needed space for employees is 120 cm.

 

Storage Needs

What kind of storage do you require? A desk with a few drawers may be attractive on the surface, but it isn’t necessarily practical. You’ll find yourself piling up paperwork and equipment on your desk, causing it to get cluttered.

A desk with many drawers, on the other hand, may make you appear rushed and clattered. It’s a waste of space and money to have empty drawers. Consider everything you’ll need to complete your everyday tasks. Calculate how much storage space you’ll need using this information.

Different departments require different storage spaces. Reflect on the everyday utilities that different departments require. IT support departments require shared and private storage for equipment and wires and larger workspaces that handle two computers – or sometimes more – at the same time, while other departments require smaller desks but bigger storage spaces for files. On the other hand, executives who have less storage requirements would just need standard set of drawers embedded in their desks.

 

Number of Employees in the Space

Space capacity vs. number of employees required to fit in the space is an important criterion prior to deciding which desk to purchase. Because the space available is constant, the desk size is one important factor and will impact the type of desk and its placement too.

Every space must have sufficient floor and unoccupied space to avoid compromising health, safety and welfare of your employees. Employees in a shared office must have enough space to access their desks and move around freely.

One optimum space utilization desk position is the wall placement position, where desks are overlooking the wall with their backs to each other. This placement – although not an authority position – allows for ample space behind the desks to move chairs at ease. Another efficient solution for shared spaces is a workstation. Workstations are designed to increase collaboration and maximize space utilization with smart storage solutions.

 

Office Décor

Buying desks that complement the rest of the office furniture and are aligned with the brand persona will help maintain the overall appearance of your office. Considering your company’s interior helps you attain a sleek and professional impression. Make sure that desk style and color complement the rest of the room’s décor and wall paint.

Starting with the central question of whether you want a traditional or contemporary style can make the process go much more smoothly and assist you in putting everything together. If your company is modern and has a young personality, consider lighter desk colors that can accommodate modern wall branding and colorful chairs. If your company is on the classic side of branding, consider dark wood desks that go well with elegant office accessories and décor.

 

Technology Requirements

What technological requirements do you have?  Do you require a desk with built-in ports to accommodate electric cords? Is it better to have the opening on the side, top, or perhaps both? Make sure you consider the above questions thoroughly. Consider how you’ll power your computer, mobile devices, and other accessories. Before making a purchase, visualize your exact technology requirements. Desks that have smart accessories avoid employees bending to plug and unplug their devices on a daily basis.

 

Desk Ergonomics

An ergonomic desk is one that is meant to keep the user from getting hurt or working uncomfortably.  Upper limb discomfort, elbow pain, neck pain, and back pain are all preventable injuries. An ergonomic workstation improves the user’s focus and productivity by providing optimal comfort. An ergonomic desk and chair should be set up at the appropriate height for the individual. Check that your desk is at the right height, invest in an adjustable desk to meet different employee preferences. Objects on the desk, such as the keyboard and mouse, should be at a comfortable distance and within easy reach and make sure your monitor is placed correctly.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title.rendered: Etisalat Egypt Selects Innovo Office Furniture For A Complete Workspace Furnishing Solution

content.rendered:
[vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text text_larger=”no”]Etisalat Egypt selected Innovo as a furnishing solution partner for the revamping of their office space furniture. With a smart blend of innovative designs, Innovo delivered 2,627 desk chairs, 3,232 workstations,169 desks and 126 silent rooms that narrate Etisalat’s brand story.

Designed with a purpose, Innovo’s office furniture solution not only amplifies Etisalat’s internal brand prevalence but also creates a more efficient work space that accommodates different departmental functionalities with smart furniture and acoustic solutions.

 

Tailoring spaces that tell a brand story, Innovo capitalized on Etisalat’s company pillars, combining brand colors, values, philosophy and organizational structure into an aesthetically appealing and functional office furniture solution.

Innovo’s project management and skilled technical teams worked for 12 days around the clock to ensure timely installation, guaranteeing uninterrupted operations. Etisalat’s main aim was to give its internal culture a makeover, making it cozier and friendlier in order to enhance staff morale and productivity.

 

Innovo capitalized on functionality, design, comfort, efficiency and ergonomics of leading European brands like Interstuhl & Narbutas to create a workspace that motivates employees to be productive and result oriented. With guaranteed high quality and endless furniture design choices, Innovo was able to furnish Etisalat offices with solutions that fit the brand and are in line with Etisalat’s friendly culture.

“Our objective is to design workplace spaces that tell brand stories in a functional, timeless, comfortable, and aesthetic way, with a flexible work strategy that delivers on time. We take pride in delivering Etisalat’s new office furniture solution timely and with the highest quality,” stated Ayman El Baz, Innovo Office Furniture Managing Director.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title.rendered: 5 Things To Consider When Buying Your Office Furniture

content.rendered:
[vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text text_larger=”no”]Many companies underestimate the importance of office furniture in getting a business up and running and reaching the ideal efficiency needed for growth. To be productive at work, comfort, ergonomic health and mood are all essential for employees’ performance and long-term health. An average employee spends at least 160 hours per month within the office space, 33% of his conscious time per month. Below are 5 things to consider when selecting your office furniture:

 

Functionality

Every company has its own functional departments. Does your company operate in a collaboration set up, where brainstorming and meetings are the main drivers of what the company serve? Does your company have to handle sensitive information that require employees to work in private set ups? Does your company have a clear-cut C-level and managerial DNA that require executive rooms and board/ conference rooms? The operational needs of your company determine the selection of your office furniture. Your organization should provide the right space for employees to shine, whether classic meeting tables for operational review, more casual meeting rooms for brainstorming, cool break-out areas to unwind of stressful operations, acoustic solutions for departments that need more controlled acoustics during the day, efficient workstations for employees who work in open spaces and managers rooms for more clear-cut C-level set ups.

The office furniture you select must also be flexible enough to accommodate the needs of each individual in your office. A good example is having desks and table solutions that can be adjusted in height so that employees can work productively while keeping correct posture. Similarly, adjustable arm, head, and backrests are extremely important features to consider in the office chairs you select. Employees’ preferences are different, so investing in furniture that can be customized to fit their needs will help keep everyone in the workplace comfortable and focused.

 

Brand aesthetics

Now is your chance to get creative, choosing furniture that reflects your brand aesthetics is a very important step to consider in your furniture selecting process.  A brand’s aesthetic is the mood and style that demonstrates your brand’s personality. Brand aesthetics include the colors, shapes and creative messages that differentiates one company from the other. If your company’s brand color is apple green, don’t just use black branding. Ensure that you introduce the brand colors in various elements of the office space, such as the chairs, the set of drawers or the wall mount branding.

Also consider materials. Furniture made of wood has a more traditional vibe, while steel and glass furniture have a more modern and industrial look and feel. Choosing a consistent aesthetic for your desks, storage cabinets, seats, and other conspicuous aspects in your office can have a significant impact on your company’s success as having a visually appealing office might help you attract clients and keep great employees who represent your company’s DNA. Matching your office’s look and feel will be easier if you pay attention to the finishes and textiles used in your furniture. Every little detail in your office contributes to its overall aesthetic.

 

Brand persona

A brand persona is just a set of personality traits, attitudes, and values that allows your audience to recognize and connect with your company. Something that reflects your individuality, style and brand image should be considered when furnishing your office. Concentrate on choosing office furniture that reflects the company’s mission, don’t be hesitant to choose trendy or out-of-the-box products if you’re a bright firm that caters to a younger clientele. If you’re catering to an older or more suit-style audience, though, aim for more sophisticated, high-end pieces. Your office reveals a lot about who you are as a company to your customers. As a result, it’s critical to find something that can help you keep your brand’s image and reputation.

 

Ergonomics

Ergonomics is the study of how to build or arrange workplaces so that they are comfortable for employees who use them. You should get ergonomic and comfortable furnishings for your office as your health, as well as the health of your staff, highly depend on it.

Every item of workplace furniture you select should take ergonomics into consideration, as ergonomics can highly affect your employees’ productivity, health, and satisfaction in a variety of ways. From footrests to adjustable monitor arms to chairs that correctly support their lower backs, Ergonomic furniture adds value to a company in a variety of ways. Employees who are healthy and comfortable are happier, more engaged, more productive than those who are distracted by a poorly constructed workplace or suffer from chronic discomfort. Choosing the right ergonomic set ups also sends one very important message to your employees: We care for you.

 

Aftercare

Once your furniture is delivered and installed, you want to make sure that the pieces you purchase are backed by a reputable company because office furniture is a significant investment. Make sure to invest in aftercare services that guarantee if you were to face an issue with your office furniture, you’ll be able to get it fixed promptly and affordably. Because after all, no company wants to compromise an uninterrupted business operation.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]
